---
title: "Raccoon"
subtitle: "AlphaZero-style backgammon AI"
---

Raccoon is a backgammon AI that uses AlphaZero-style reinforcement learning to challenge GNUBG at money game play. The name is a nod to the doubling cube term in backgammon.

GNUBG, the strongest open-source backgammon engine, uses TD reinforcement learning with value-only neural networks and shallow search — a design from the 1990s (TD-Gammon era). Modern AlphaZero-style training (policy+value network + Monte Carlo Tree Search) has beaten world-class engines in chess, shogi, and Go. This project applies that approach to backgammon.

## Quick Start

```bash
make setup          # Install in editable mode with dev deps
make test           # Run all tests
make smoke          # Quick sanity: 2 iterations, 3 games, 10 sims
make train          # Full training run
make play           # Play against Raccoon in terminal
```

Training with custom parameters (`--experiment-name` is required; outputs go to `experiments/<name>/`):

```bash
python3 scripts/train.py --experiment-name my-run --iterations 100 \
  --games-per-iter 50 --simulations 100 --lr 0.001 \
  --channels 128 --num-blocks 6 --checkpoint-every 10
```

## Project Map

| Doc | What it covers |
|---|---|
| [Architecture](architecture.md) | Board encoding, network, MCTS, and training loop design |
| [MCTS Explained](mcts_explained.md) | Concepts: simulations, PUCT selection, temperature, Dirichlet noise |
| [Training Parameters](training_parameters.md) | Every CLI flag explained with intuition and reference points |
| [Self-Play Analysis](training_analysis.qmd) | The self-play track (exp000–exp007): what pure AlphaZero self-play achieved, and why it plateaued |
| [Supervised & Expert-Iteration Analysis](pretraining_analysis.qmd) | The supervised track: distilling GNUBG (v1–v5), on-distribution DAgger, consolidation — the current best results |
| [GCP Guide](gcp_guide.md) | Running training on Google Cloud GPU VMs |

## Tech Stack

- **Python 3.10+**, **PyTorch** — neural network training
- **OpenSpiel** — backgammon game logic and 1352-action encoding
- **NumPy** — tensor encoding
- **pytest** — testing
- **GNUBG** — evaluation benchmark (CLI mode)

## Source

[github.com/lassehjorthmadsen/raccoon](https://github.com/lassehjorthmadsen/raccoon)
